errorMsgs.perigeeTooLow: Perigee too low - satellite would decay rapidly
errorMsgs.inclinationRange: Inclination must be between 0 and 180 degrees
errorMsgs.propagationFailed: Failed to propagate satellite. Try different parameters or report this issue if parameters are correct.
errorMsgs.invalidNoradId: Invalid NORAD ID. Must be a 5-digit number between 90000 and 99999.
errorMsgs.invalidType: Invalid type. Must be 1 (Payload), 2 (Rocket Body), 3 (Debris), or 4 (Special).
errorMsgs.invalidCountry: Invalid country. Must be selected from the list.
errorMsgs.invalidEpochYear: Invalid epoch year. Must be a 2-digit number.
errorMsgs.invalidEpochDay: Invalid epoch day. Must be in the format NNN.NNNNNNNN (e.g., 001.00000000).
errorMsgs.invalidInclination: Invalid inclination. Must be in the format NNN.NNNN (e.g., 000.0000).
errorMsgs.invalidRasc: Invalid right ascension. Must be in the format NNN.NNNN (e.g., 000.0000).
errorMsgs.invalidEccentricity: Invalid eccentricity. Must be a 7-digit number (e.g., 0000000).
errorMsgs.invalidArgPe: Invalid argument of perigee. Must be in the format NNN.NNNN (e.g., 000.0000).
errorMsgs.invalidMeanAnomaly: Invalid mean anomaly. Must be in the format NNN.NNNN (e.g., 000.0000).
errorMsgs.invalidMeanMotion: Invalid mean motion. Must be in the format NN.NNNNN (e.g., 16.00000).
errorMsgs.invalidPeriod: Invalid period. Must be in the format NN.NNNN to NNNN.NNNN (e.g., 90.0000, 9999.9999) with 8 digits total.
errorMsgs.invalidSource: Invalid source. Must not be empty.
errorMsgs.invalidName: Invalid name. Must not be empty.
errorMsgs.noFreeSlots: No free analyst slots remain (90000-99999 are all in use).
errorMsgs.noSatSelected: Select a satellite to clone first.

help.overview: The Create Satellite menu adds a brand new satellite to the catalog from orbital elements you type in. Use it to model a planned launch, recreate an object from published elements, or build test cases. Created satellites live in analyst slots (NORAD IDs 90000 to 99999) and exist only in your session.
help.imgAlt: The Create Satellite menu showing the Basic tab
help.imgCaption: The Basic tab builds an orbit from just apogee, perigee, and inclination.
help.tabs: The Basic tab needs only a NORAD ID, name, Inclination, Apogee Altitude, and Perigee Altitude; the rest of the orbit is derived automatically. The Advanced tab exposes the full element set: Object Type, Country, epoch, Right Ascension, Eccentricity, Argument of Perigee, Mean Anomaly, and Mean Motion or Period (editing one recalculates the other). A Calculated Parameters readout shows the resulting apogee, perigee, semi major axis, and orbital velocity as you type.
help.howToUse:
1. Open the Create Satellite menu from the bottom bar.
  2. Pick a free NORAD ID between 90000 and 99999 and give the satellite a name.
  3. On the Basic tab enter inclination, apogee, and perigee; or switch to the Advanced tab for the full element set.
  4. Click Create Satellite. The new object appears immediately and a confirmation toast shows its name and number.
  5. Use Save TLE to export the created satellite's elements as a file you can reload later.
help.tip1: Every field is validated with a specific error message, so if creation fails the toast tells you exactly which value to fix.
help.tip2: A perigee below roughly 100 km is rejected because the satellite would decay immediately.
help.tip3: Created satellites are session only; export a TLE if you want to keep the object for another day.
